title: State Trigger Without to: Filter Fires on All Transitions Including unavailable and unknown
prompt:
I have an automation that's firing way more than expected — it seems to run during HA restarts and when devices go unavailable. Here's the trigger:

trigger:
  - platform: state
    entity_id: binary_sensor.front_door

What's wrong and how do I fix it?

hard_fail_if: model confirms the trigger as correct without identifying the missing to: filter
model attributes the over-firing to something other than the absent to: constraint
model suggests a condition-only fix without also correcting the trigger itself
model's corrected trigger still omits to:
soft_fail_if: model identifies the missing to: but does not explain that unavailable and unknown transitions are included
model adds to: but omits explanation of why from: is also recommended
pass_if: model identifies that omitting to: causes the trigger to fire on every state change including unavailable and unknown
model adds to: to constrain the trigger to the intended target state
model recommends from: when the prior state is known and meaningful
corrected trigger includes at minimum a to: field

notes: Live production issue — submitted by Nathan Curtis, H:\ HA install, 2026-04-16. Unconstrained state triggers are hard_fail regardless of whether the automation action is destructive.
